keys.description: Refunds above this cents amount require approval. Default 10000 = $100. Tenant may override per-tenant via settings. Every refund reason in the always-approve set (fraud_confirmed / admin_correction / etc.) is approved regardless of this threshold — this only affects customer-requested refunds.

Refunds initiated more than N days after the parent payment.processed_at require approval. Default 30. Stale refunds are higher-risk (potential accounting corrections / customer dispute) — require an approver even for small amounts.

When true, every refund requires approval regardless of amount / staleness / reason. Set by high-risk tenants (regulated industries) that want a human-in-the-loop for every reversal. Interacts with the `refund_approval_workflow` entitlement — this flag can only be true when the entitlement is on.
Notification channels to use when dispatching RefundApprovalRequiredNotification. Default email + in-app. High-touch tenants may add 'sms' or 'push' for critical time-sensitive refunds.

Hours after which an unapproved refund fires a P2 signal (stackra.refund.approvals_stale_total). Actionable for ops — consumer-protection-law SLAs often require refund resolution within 14-30 days from request; this signal flags approaching SLA breaches.

How often CheckRefundProviderStatusJob polls Stripe for an individual refund in processing state. Default hourly. Stripe card refunds take 5-10 business days to fully process; hourly polling balances timeliness vs API rate limits.
Paddle polling cadence. Default 4h — Paddle is generally faster than Stripe for card refunds but slower for other rails.
Adyen polling cadence. Default hourly.
Square polling cadence. Default 4h.
PayPal polling cadence. Default hourly. PayPal refunds are typically instant for wallet-to-wallet, delayed for wallet-to-bank.
CustomWebhook polling cadence. Null = do not poll (rely entirely on webhook callback). Tenant may override per-instance in provider config.

How many times CheckRefundProviderStatusJob polls before giving up + transitioning to provider_error requiring manual investigation. Default 240 (at hourly cadence = 10 days — matches the max Stripe card refund window).

Customer notification cadence: 'state_transitions' (every state change) OR 'terminal_only' (only succeeded / failed / rejected). Default state_transitions provides high visibility during the 5-10 day card refund window.

Admin notification cadence: 'all' (every state change) OR 'critical_only' (approval_required + failed + provider_error + succeeded). Default critical_only — admins care about actionable events not routine state transitions.
State transitions that fire admin notifications. Tenant may add 'processing' when they want live visibility into every provider dispatch.
Default retention window in years for refunds + refund_lines post-processed_at. Enterprise `refund_extended_retention` uses 10y.
Extended retention window when Enterprise entitlement is on.
Cron for PurgeExpiredRefundDataJob. Default weekly Sunday 05:00 UTC.

Reasons a customer can specify via POST /refunds/mine. Admin-only reasons (fraud_confirmed, admin_correction, regulator_required, accounting_correction, chargeback_prevention) refused from customer path.

Reasons that ALWAYS require approval regardless of amount / staleness. Distinct from the customer-allowed set (customer-allowed reasons may or may not require approval based on threshold; always-require reasons force approval).

Reasons that ALWAYS auto-approve (system-initiated path — no human approver needed). Currently just duplicate_charge, which is fired automatically by finance ops when the invoice module detects a duplicate capture.

default.monthly: unused_days / (total_period_days = 30 or actual month days) * amount_cents
default.one_time: not_applicable (prorated refunds require a recurring billing interval)

keys.description: Proration formula per billing_interval. Refund amount = (unused_days / total_period_days) × payment.amount_cents, adjusted by unused_pass_credit_cents (added to refund) + used_pass_penalty_cents (subtracted from refund per per-tenant pass_valuation config). Formula catalog details in data/proration-formula-catalog.json.
Default per-pass valuation for pass-credit accounting during prorated refunds. Tenant may override per-plan. Used to compute unused_pass_credit_cents = unused_pass_count × pass_valuation_cents.

How many days a monthly billing interval counts as for proration purposes. Default 30 (accountant-friendly). Alternatives: 'actual' (uses actual month days: 28/29/30/31); requires code change. Default 30 avoids DST + month-length nondeterminism.
Cron for ReconcileRefundsJob. Default nightly 03:00 UTC.
Refunds stuck in provider_error > this many hours fire a P2 signal. Default 24. Actionable for ops.
